title: Tom Brady referenced Super Bowl comeback, Bill Belichick jokes in graduation speech
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Tom Brady delivered a graduation speech at Georgetown University's McDonough School of Business, emphasizing resilience and perseverance.
Brady recounted the Patriots' Super Bowl LI comeback against the Falcons, highlighting the 99.7% win probability against them.
He humorously referenced his relationship with Bill Belichick, encouraging graduates to welcome challenges and tough mentorships.

title: Here’s how much a finance manager makes in Mansfield
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: A finance manager in Mansfield, MA, earns $120,000 annually while living in Cumberland, RI.
The finance manager's main expenses include a $2,200 mortgage and $1,100 car payment.
He works hybrid in telecommunications, drawn by visible positive impacts on the local community.

title: Livestream: Opening statements in rape trial of Alvin Campbell Jr., AG’s brother
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Alvin Campbell Jr. is on trial for allegedly sexually assaulting nine women between 2017 and 2019 in Boston.
Campbell, accused of posing as a rideshare driver, faces charges of rape, kidnapping, and indecent assault and battery.
Massachusetts AG Andrea Campbell, Alvin's sister, expressed condolences to survivors and respect for the judicial process.

title: Jaylen Brown curses out Stephen A. Smith, calls on ESPN pundit to retire once again
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown criticized Stephen A. Smith during a Twitch stream, calling for Smith's retirement and accusing him of 'clickbait' journalism.
The feud was sparked by Smith's commentary on Brown's statement that the 2025-26 season was his favorite, despite a playoff exit.
Smith suggested Jayson Tatum's absence from Brown's stream appeared indicative of tension, which Brown rejected as a false narrative.

title: Condo in CT’s once-controversial ‘spaceship’ building lists for 779k
author: Megan Johnson
bullets: A condo in Guilford, CT's 'Spaceship' building is listed for $779,000, featuring one bedroom and two bathrooms.
Designed by architect Wilfred Armster, the controversial 1980s building contrasts with surrounding Colonial and Victorian styles.
The 1,672 sq. ft. unit offers views of Long Island Sound, a two-car garage, and modern updates including new HVAC systems.

title: Lynn teacher was drugged and assaulted by coworker, lawsuit claims
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: A Lynn teacher filed a lawsuit claiming she was drugged and sexually assaulted by a male colleague in April 2025.
The lawsuit asserts Lynn Public Schools failed to promptly investigate the incident and allowed the alleged attacker to remain employed.
The accused colleague had previously been acquitted of indecent assault; Lynn Public Schools is being sued for over $500,000.

title: Exonerated Chelsea man is suing for $1 million after 34 years in prison
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Thomas Rosa, exonerated after 34 years in prison, sues Massachusetts for $1 million and additional compensation.
Rosa was convicted in 1993 for murder, kidnapping, and assault; his convictions were vacated due to new DNA evidence.
Rosa's lawsuit alleges Boston police misconduct, including fabricating information and ignoring evidence pointing to other suspects.

title: Ranking the Patriots’ 2026 schedule from least to most exciting games
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Patriots will face the sixth-hardest 2026 schedule with games against eight 2025 playoff teams like Seattle and Buffalo.
New England's 2026 schedule includes international and West Coast games, a change from last year's lighter travel demands.
Key matches include primetime games versus Seahawks, Chargers, and Chiefs, with playoff implications likely against the Broncos.

title: Lakeville police investigating pipe bomb found near town pond
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Pipe bomb found near Egger Bog Pond in Lakeville, discovered by a resident near Highland Road.
Massachusetts State Police Bomb Squad confirmed and safely disposed of the PVC pipe bomb.
Lakeville Police are investigating; information can be reported to Detective Robert Schiffer or the Detective Division.

title: Mass. McDonald’s employee to face criminal charges after viral video shows alleged food contamination
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: A Southbridge McDonald’s employee seen contaminating food in a viral video will face criminal charges, police announced.
The employee consumed fries and returned them to a container, allegedly intending to serve them to customers.
The McDonald's franchise in Southbridge has terminated those involved and is cooperating with local authorities for further actions.
